name: Bahamas

colors.symbolism: the aquamarine waters of the Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea that surround the islands
the golden sand of the beaches and the country's other natural resources
the strength, vigour and force of a united people

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — aquamarine, gold (slightly narrower at 1:2:1 by some renderings, equal in others) and aquamarine — with a black equilateral triangle resting on the hoist, its apex pointing toward the centre of the flag.
history: Adopted on 10 July 1973, the day the Bahamas gained independence from the United Kingdom. The design was selected from over a hundred entries in a national competition; the colours and the eastward-pointing triangle were intended to suggest a young nation moving forward to harness its land and sea resources.

features.symbolism: the aquamarine stripes evoke the surrounding sea while the gold band represents the islands' beaches and natural resources
the strength and determination of the Bahamian people, directed toward the development of the rich natural resources symbolised by the sun and the sea
